items.q: How do you say 'I like coffee' in French?
items.why: English drops articles for general likes/dislikes; French keeps them. For general preferences, use the DEFINITE article: j'aime le chocolat, je déteste les épinards.

items.q: Which mood follows 'je souhaite que' (I wish that)?
items.why: Students use indicative after expressions of wish. SOUHAITER QUE, VOULOIR QUE, AIMER QUE all trigger subjunctive when there's a different subject. Note: espérer que typically takes indicative - a common irregularity.

items.q: What is the 'vous' form of 'être' in the present tense?
items.why: Students apply -ez ending from regular verbs. Être is irregular: je suis, tu es, il est, nous sommes, vous ÊTES, ils sont. Memorisation is essential - être appears constantly.

items.q: Which sentence correctly says 'I get on well with my brother'?
items.correct: Je m'entends bien avec mon frère.
items.misconception: Je m'entend bien avec mon frère.
items.random_wrong: Je entends bien avec mon frère.
items.why: It's Je m'entends bien avec mon frère.. “Je m'entend bien avec mon frère.” is the classic near-miss here; knowing why it's wrong is worth as much as knowing the right answer.

items.q: Which form of 'you' should you use when addressing your French teacher?
items.why: Correct answer: vous. “tu” sounds right because it borrows the language of the topic — but it fails this question. Re-read the notes on why.

items.q: How many forms does the French adjective 'beau' have (masc sing, masc plur, fem sing, fem plur, masc before vowel 'bel')?
items.why: Exact answer: 5 forms. Estimation gets you near it; only the anchored number survives the exam hall.
